query: Has the NFL placed Terrion Arnold on the Commissioner's Exempt List as of July 2026? Latest news on Detroit Lions cornerback Terrion Arnold NFL status after June 2026 arrest.
answer:
## Summary

**No — the NFL did NOT place Terrion Arnold on the Commissioner's Exempt List.** Instead, the **Detroit Lions released/waived him on June 29, 2026.**

Key findings:
- **June ~24, 2026**: Arnold was arrested on **8 felony counts** (4 kidnapping, 4 armed robbery) stemming from February 2026 incidents in Tampa, FL, where he allegedly orchestrated a kidnapping/robbery of three men.
- **June 29, 2026**: A Florida judge set his bond at **$1 million** with conditions and allowed him to practice/play. **Hours later, the Lions waived him** (reported by ESPN's Adam Schefter).
- **Exempt List does not apply**: Because Detroit released him outright rather than retaining him, the Commissioner's Exempt List mechanism (which requires a player be under contract) is moot. Pro Football Rumors explicitly notes the Lions "opted to release him outright." Spotrac's transaction log confirms: "Terrion Arnold (CB) — Jun 29, 2026 — Waived by Detroit."
- **Current status**: Expected to clear waivers unclaimed and remain an unsigned free agent while his legal case (potential life sentence) proceeds. He is charged but not convicted.

All major outlets (ESPN, CBS, NBC/PFT, Pro Football Rumors, Spotrac, Yahoo) are consistent on this. The answer to the specific question is definitively **negative**: he's a released free agent, not on the exempt list.

sources.title: Lions Waive CB Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: The Lions have waived cornerback Terrion Arnold, per a team announcement. Arnold was not placed on the Commissioner's Exempt List; Detroit opted to release him outright.

sources.title: Lions release Terrion Arnold after CB's arrest on felony charges
sources.snippet: Detroit released Arnold on June 29, 2026, after a judge set bond at $1 million. He had two years and $4.8M remaining on his rookie contract.

sources.title: Lions decide to part ways with Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: Lions waived CB Terrion Arnold. Arnold will now go on waivers, where he will presumably go unclaimed, and is likely to remain unsigned while he works through his legal issues.

sources.title: NFL player charged with kidnapping gets 'tremendous benefit' in ruling to keep job
sources.snippet: Update: The Detroit Lions announced they released Terrion Arnold on Monday, June 29, hours after a judge granted him bail.

sources.title: Lions release Terrion Arnold after arrest on kidnapping, armed robbery charges; CB faces life in prison
sources.snippet: The Detroit Lions have released cornerback Terrion Arnold less than a week after being arrested in Florida on multiple felonies including four counts of kidnapping.

sources.title: Why the Detroit Lions cut Terrion Arnold now
sources.snippet: On Monday, hours after a Florida judge set Terrion Arnold's bond at $1 million, the Detroit Lions cut ties with the 23-year-old cornerback.

sources.title: Next Step for Terrion Arnold Revealed After Lions Cut Ties With CB
sources.snippet: Arnold is a cornerback for the Detroit Lions no more; his path forward in the NFL is murky. He heads to the waiver wire and likely free agency.
